dialogues: The Minis are here. Let's count what we see. Use the counter to keep track of each group.
Let's check your counts. There were [X] [category A], [Y] [category B], and [Z] [category C]. Now we've got our data.
Let's make a picture graph with your data. We're using stars this time. See how each star stands for 1 item.
Watch how I show Red on the graph. You counted 5 Red. So I'm adding 5 symbols. One... two... three... four... five. There. 5 symbols for Red. Each symbol stands for 1 item.
Your turn. You counted 5 Cats. Add 5 cat symbols -- one for each Cat you counted.
5 cat symbols. One for each Cat.
Now Yellow. You counted 8. Click to add 8 stars.
That's 8 stars. You made a picture graph.
You made a picture graph with your data. Graphs can show all kinds of information. Ready to see what else we can learn from them?
